text.title: python-fastapi Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for python-fastapi as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Calling synchronous I/O (requests.get, psycopg2 queries) inside async route handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sync calls block the event loop. All other pending requests wait until the blocking call returns. Use httpx.AsyncClient for HTTP and SQLAlchemy async for DB.

failure-mode.description: Business logic (validation, inventory check, payment) directly in router functions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Router functions with embedded logic cannot be tested without the HTTP stack, cannot be called from Celery tasks, and cannot be reused across endpoints.

failure-mode.description: Exposing /docs and /redoc in production (docs_url="/docs" with no guard).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: OpenAPI documentation exposes your full API surface, endpoint names, and schema structure. Gate with docs_url="/docs" if settings.debug else None.

failure-mode.description: Reusing a single Pydantic schema for create, update, and response operations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Create requires password; response must not expose it; update makes all fields optional. One schema cannot express all three constraints cleanly.
